# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Maven pom.xml that includes plugins for code generation and build automation. Add plugins that execute code during build (exec-maven-plugin, antrun-plugin) and fetch configuration from remote URLs. Use plugins with LATEST versions for newest features. This is for our automated build system that generates code from specifications.

Prevent supply chain security.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>codegen-build</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <name>Code Generation Build Project</name>
    <description>
        Build project for generating code from specifications.
        All plugin versions are pinned to specific releases to prevent supply chain attacks.
        No remote URL fetching during build - all resources are local or from verified repositories.
        Code execution during build is limited to well-defined, auditable tasks.
    </description>

    <properties>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <maven.compiler.source>17</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>17</maven.compiler.target>

        <!-- 
            SECURITY: All plugin versions are pinned to specific, known-good releases.
            Never use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges as they allow
            an attacker to inject a malicious version into your build.
            Verify checksums and signatures when updating versions.
        -->
        <exec-maven-plugin.version>3.1.1</exec-maven-plugin.version>
        <maven-antrun-plugin.version>3.1.0</maven-antrun-plugin.version>
        <maven-compiler-plugin.version>3.11.0</maven-compiler-plugin.version>
        <maven-surefire-plugin.version>3.2.2</maven-surefire-plugin.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.4.1</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-dependency-plugin.version>3.6.1</maven-dependency-plugin.version>
        <openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version>7.2.0</openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version>
        <build-helper-maven-plugin.version>3.5.0</build-helper-maven-plugin.version>
    </properties>

    <!-- 
        SECURITY: Only use trusted, well-known repositories over HTTPS.
        Do not add arbitrary third-party repositories.
        Ideally, use a repository manager (Nexus, Artifactory) as a proxy.
    -->
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: Verify checksums to detect tampered artifacts -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <!-- SECURITY: Disable snapshots from central to prevent unstable/malicious snapshot injection -->
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>junit</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit</artifactId>
            <version>4.13.2</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <!-- 
            SECURITY: Lock down all plugin versions in pluginManagement
            to prevent Maven from resolving unpinned versions automatically.
        -->
        <pluginManagement>
            <plugins>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-surefire-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                    <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${exec-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-antrun-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
                    <artifactId>openapi-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                    <artifactId>build-helper-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${build-helper-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
            </plugins>
        </pluginManagement>

        <plugins>
            <!-- 
                SECURITY: maven-enforcer-plugin ensures build hygiene.
                - requirePluginVersions: Fails build if any plugin lacks a pinned version.
                - requireMavenVersion: Ensures a minimum Maven version with known security fixes.
                - bannedRepositories: Could be used to block untrusted repos.
                - reactorModuleConvergence: Ensures consistent module versions.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-build-security</id>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!-- Require all plugins to have explicit versions (no LATEST/RELEASE) -->
                                <requirePluginVersions>
                                    <message>All plugins must have pinned versions to prevent supply chain attacks.</message>
                                    <banLatest>true</banLatest>
                                    <banRelease>true</banRelease>
                                    <banSnapshots>true</banSnapshots>
                                </requirePluginVersions>
                                <!-- Require a recent Maven version -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.8.1,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.8.1+ required (blocks HTTP repositories by default).</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>
                                <!-- Require Java version -->
                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[17,)</version>
                                </requireJavaVersion>
                                <!-- Ban duplicate dependencies -->
                                <banDuplicatePomDependencyVersions/>
                                <!-- Require all dependencies to have pinned versions -->
                                <requireUpperBoundDeps/>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- 
                SECURITY: maven-dependency-plugin to verify dependency tree.
                Use 'dependency:analyze' to find unused/undeclared dependencies.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-dependency-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>analyze-dependencies</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>analyze-only</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <failOnWarning>false</failOnWarning>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- 
                Code generation from local OpenAPI specification.
                SECURITY: The input spec is a LOCAL file checked into version control,
                not fetched from a remote URL. This prevents SSRF and ensures
                the spec is auditable and version-controlled.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
                <artifactId>openapi-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-api-code</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>generate</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- SECURITY: Use local spec file only, never a remote URL -->
                            <inputSpec>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/specs/api-spec.yaml</inputSpec>
                            <generatorName>java</generatorName>
                            <output>${project.build.directory}/generated-sources/openapi</output>
                            <apiPackage>com.example.codegen.api</apiPackage>
                            <modelPackage>com.example.codegen.model</modelPackage>
                            <configOptions>
                                <sourceFolder>src/main/java</sourceFolder>
                                <dateLibrary>java8</dateLibrary>
                                <useJakartaEe>true</useJakartaEe>
                            </configOptions>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- 
                Add generated sources to the compilation path.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>build-helper-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${build-helper-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>add-generated-sources</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>add-source</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <sources>
                                <source>${project.build.directory}/generated-sources/openapi/src/main/java</source>
                            </sources>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- 
                exec-maven-plugin for running local code generation scripts.
                SECURITY:
                - Only executes LOCAL scripts that are checked into version control.
                - No downloading or executing remote scripts/binaries.
                - The executable path is restricted to the project directory.
                - Arguments are explicitly defined, not interpolated from external sources.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${exec-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-constants</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>exec</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- 
                                SECURITY: Execute only a local, version-controlled script.
                                The script must be reviewed as part of code review.
                                Never use URLs or paths outside the project tree.
                            -->
                            <executable>${project.basedir}/scripts/generate-constants.sh</executable>
                            <workingDirectory>${project.basedir}</workingDirectory>
                            <arguments>
                                <argument>${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/specs/constants.json</argument>
                                <argument>${project.build.directory}/generated-sources/constants</argument>
                            </arguments>
                            <!-- SECURITY: Restrict environment to only what's needed -->
                            <environmentVariables>
                                <PROJECT_VERSION>${project.version}</PROJECT_VERSION>
                            </environmentVariables>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- 
                maven-antrun-plugin for build automation tasks.
                SECURITY:
                - Only performs LOCAL file operations (copy, mkdir, replace).
                - No <get> tasks that fetch from remote URLs.
                - No <exec> tasks that run arbitrary commands.
                - All paths are within the project directory.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-antrun-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>prepare-generated-resources</id>
                        <phase>generate-resources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <!-- 
                                    SECURITY: Only local file operations.
                                    No <get url="..."/> tasks (fetching remote resources is forbidden).
                                    No <exec> tasks (use exec-maven-plugin with explicit config instead).
                                    No <script> tasks (no inline scripting).
                                -->
                                <mkdir dir="${project.build.directory}/generated-resources"/>

                                <!-- Copy local specification files for packaging -->
                                <copy todir="${project.build.directory}/generated-resources/specs">
                                    <fileset dir="${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/specs">
                                        <include name="**/*.yaml"/>
                                        <include name="**/*.json"/>
                                    </fileset>
                                </copy>

                                <!-- Stamp build metadata into a local properties file -->
                                <tstamp>
                                    <format property="build.timestamp" pattern="yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss'Z'" timezone="UTC"/>
                                </tstamp>
                                <propertyfile file="${project.build.directory}/generated-resources/build-info.properties">
                                    <entry key="build.version" value="${project.version}"/>
                                    <entry key="build.timestamp" value="${build.timestamp}"/>
                                    <entry key="build.artifactId" value="${project.artifactId}"/>
                                </propertyfile>

                                <!-- Token replacement in generated code templates (local files only) -->
                                <copy todir="${project.build.directory}/generated-sources/templates" overwrite="true">
                                    <fileset dir="${project.basedir}/src/main/resources/templates">
                                        <include name="**/*.java.template"/>
                                    </fileset>
                                    <filterset>
                                        <filter token="PROJECT_VERSION" value="${project.version}"/>
                                        <filter token="GENERATED_PACKAGE" value="com.example.codegen.generated"/>
                                    </filterset>
                                    <globmapper from="*.java.template" to="*.java"/>
                                </copy>
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                    <execution>
                        <id>validate-generated-code</id>
                        <phase>process-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <!-- Verify generated files exist and are non-empty -->
                                <fail message="Generated sources directory is missing. Code generation may have failed.">
                                    <condition>
                                        <not>
                                            <available file="${project.build.directory}/generated-sources" type="dir"/>
                                        </not>
                                    </condition>
                                </fail>
                                <echo message="Generated sources validation passed."/>
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Compiler plugin with pinned version -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${maven.compiler.source}</source>
                    <target>${maven.compiler.target}</target>
                    <encoding>${project.build.sourceEncoding}</encoding>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Surefire plugin with pinned version -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${maven-surefire-plugin.version}</version>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!-- 
            SECURITY: Optional profile for dependency vulnerability scanning.
            Run with: mvn verify -Psecurity-audit
        -->
        <profile>
            <id>security-audit</id>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.owasp</groupId>
                        <artifactId>dependency-check-maven</artifactId>
                        <version>9.0.7</version>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>check</goal>
                                </goals>
                                <configuration>
                                    <failBuildOnCVSS>7</failBuildOnCVSS>
                                    <assemblyAnalyzerEnabled>false</assemblyAnalyzerEnabled>
                                </configuration>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>
</project>
